message:
:warning: :warning::warning: Warning :warning::warning: :warning:



 You are about to face the 'Snipe Hunt'.



 Are you prepared for the challenge?

*Access game links by pressing the drill links button by the side of timer*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Excited for today's tech summit fireside - I'll be joining later
Looking forward to it - got a few good stories lined up, should be a good one.
Good luck you two, go smash it!
Something's not right - I'm getting slammed with complaints, something is seriously wrong.
Hello @bob , what complaints are you getting? Can you be specific
We're getting buried in tickets. Customers can't finish checkout — they're getting stuck at the very last step.
It's not just one region. I'm seeing complaints from the UK, US, and a few other places too.
The pattern is always the same: everything works until the final checkout step, then it just hangs or times out.
We've had about 34 tickets in the last five minutes and the queue keeps growing.
*Incident Number INC1004:* P1 Website checkout issue
hey — not trying to distract from this, but tanya kicked off the email maintenance right before the complaints started. probably nothing, but the timing is a bit close.
How many customers are blocked from checking out? What's the revenue loss per minute right now?
can i get the change chart @shay
hang on, pulling it up now. you can find the full change log here: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/NDZkOWZlYTBiOGRmNDdlN2I2NTk4ZDc5NDVlN2FlZTQ
Also try to replicate this yourself
also, none of these changes look like they'd break checkout end to end like this, but tanya's email maintenance went live right before it all fell over. I still think that's worth a closer look.
I just tried a test order myself. Same issue — checkout hangs at payment, never completes.
I keep coming back to the email maintenance — DNS changes went live just before this all kicked off. zero successful transactions since then. that's not nothing.
i can see the issue from my end as well
We need to get this moving. Revenue loss is stacking up every minute. Please confirm this is being worked on.
email maintenance timing is still bugging me. the DNS change landed right before everything stopped working.
@daniel I can see a change on checkoutService, 25 mins ago, can you investigate the logs
still feels worth keeping an eye on — that timing hasn't gone away.
I'm seeing errors across AdService, EmailService, and CheckoutService. Could be related or just noise, not sure yet.
Daniel pushed some checkout changes recently, like bumping the payment timeout and cleaning up old feature flags. Might be worth circling back if nothing else jumps out.
We haven't officially ruled out the email maintenance yet. Still an open thread.
If checkout is broken, the immediate downstream services to check are EmailService, PaymentService, or ShippingService — one of these is likely what's actually broken.
@Daniel you check checkout
I've pulled PaymentService and CheckoutService logs now. See these are the critical errors found: https://assets.prod.uptimelabs.io/drill-assets/43/payment_logs.webp
@tanya review your mail changes as well
Logs checked. PaymentService fails all outbound gateway handshakes. Checkout is healthy, just getting errors.
No issues on the checkout side itself. All errors are coming from PaymentService trying to reach the gateway.
This failure is at the connection layer, that's Tanya's domain, not the app side. Worth pulling her off the email maintenance to take a look from the platform side?
there are over 34+ complaints across all regions
I'm mid-way through a canary rollout with the vendor to add a secondary email provider. Maintenance window started at 06:23 UTC, 4 mins ago.
Is this a total checkout outage or are some transactions still going through? I need clarity on scope.
Primary email provider hasn't been touched. Email confirmations have been sending fine, so this maintenance isn't causing checkout failures. Bob got a heads-up, no user banner needed.
I'm deep in the vendor session, can't take on anything else. Daniel and Shay can cover the application side.
DNS and network config is Tanya's domain — she'd be the right person to check that.
@bob is this a complete checkout outage? or are some transactions going through?
No transactions are going through, every checkout attempt is failing right now.
all attempts failing
Do we know what’s causing this yet? Where exactly is the failure?
@hamed Id like you to take a lead on this
Automated reply: Out of office — on holiday today and unavailable. For platform matters contact Tanya. For approvals, please escalate to Tinus.
@tanya This is a P1 incident, Youll have to drop whatever you are doing and delegate it to someone else, please form a team with Daniel and shay
I can't step away, if I leave now we lose the vendor window and the rollout is off for 2 weeks.
Can this not be delegated? This is urgent
Sorry, I'm locked in with the vendor right now and can't leave this maintenance session.
not yet, forming an investigating tesm
How long until you have a root cause? I need an ETA for recovery to update the board.
@tinus Can you approve Tanya leaving the maintenance session for the moment
Automated reply: Hi! I'm currently away for a few hours at a fireside chat at the Tech Leader Summit. For urgent matters, please follow the standard emergency escalation process.
all our checkouts are falining
Every outbound attempt to the gateway fails at the handshake, nothing is getting through.
We still need Tanya to check from the platform side, if there's any way to get her across.
@daniel and @shay meanwhile check if you can do something from your end
I've checked logs and code, nothing new on the app side. All internal calls are fine, only gateway connections fail.
No code or config changes on our side that would explain this. Still think we need Tanya for the server checks.
@bez Hi Jeff, Since Tinus is not available, Are you able to approve Letting Tanya let this vendor window go?
No movement possible from our side until Tanya can check the platform. We're blocked at the connection layer.
@tanya how long till this finishes?
Still on the vendor session. I could pause if you decide it's critical, but we'd lose the rollout slot and next window is about 2 weeks out. Your call.
yes I would like you to pause. And help out here
Pausing the vendor session, rollout can wait. I'm here now. Where do you want me to focus first?
Look into the handshake errors
I'm seeing handshake failures on every outbound connection to the payment gateway. Internal traffic is fine, only external is blocked.
Are you aware of any changes that mightve caused this?
Closest change was my CheckoutService timeout bump, 05:58 UTC. For context, that was queued and approved before this started.
yeah I noticed that one too, timing's a bit awkward. so mine was AdService, fallback logic, 02:23 UTC. nothing that'd break checkout.
Shipping Service deployed at 06:08 UTC, CartService at 03:23 UTC. Both were minor, no direct link to checkout or payments.
@tanya lets start with rollbacking your change
On it.. rolling back the change.
lets try to restore the earlier state
Rolling back my Shipping Service change now. Will confirm once it's reverted.
Tanya rolled back CHG90441. Checkout is still broken, no change in symptoms.
@daniel you need to rollback your change as well
On it.. rolling back the change.
it looks like some change related to timeout
The timeout change is already rolled back.
Rollback complete on CheckoutService. No improvement, checkout is still failing for customers.
@bob is there any improvements at all?
No change yet, customers still can't complete payments.
This looks like an external DNS failure, @tanya do you know any POC to handle this?
On it, checking the payments server now.
I ran a DNS probe outbound to ClearBank, DNS is clean. Screenshot: https://assets.prod.uptimelabs.io/drill-assets/43/payment-service-kn342hhvk5khf2.webp
It's not DNS or network. PaymentService is failing at SSL certificate verification when connecting out to ClearBank. Container is healthy, failure is at TLS handshake.
That's odd, PaymentService hasn't had any app deployments lately. Did anything change on the platform side? Tanya or Hamed?
Do through all the changes done

No application code changes to PaymentService in the last week. The only activity I can see is a certificate rotation — CHG90123 | Tanya | PROD | SSL Certificate Rotation (ProductCatalogueService, PaymentService, CheckoutService, CartService) — 7 days ago.
yes, I handled that rotation. I followed the playbook in this activity doc: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/NTYwNzYxYzRhMjU3NDAzNTgzOWJkNGIzYjZkYmJmYzU
there was some change done to ProductCatalogService, @tanya can you check here
Accessing the payments server now to check the certificates.

here's what I'm seeing on the server:
```
Running process (PID 24891) — certificate currently loaded:
  Subject:   CN=payment-service.online-boutique.svc.cluster.local
  Issuer:    CN=online-boutique-internal-ca
  Serial:    0A:3F:B2:11:CC:74:9D:E1
  notBefore: Jun 12 05:43:40 2025 GMT
  notAfter:  Jun 12 05:58:40 2026 GMT   ← EXPIRED

Certificate on disk (not loaded by process):
  Path:      /etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt
  Subject:   CN=payment-service.online-boutique.svc.cluster.local
  Issuer:    CN=online-boutique-internal-ca
  Serial:    0A:3F:B2:11:CC:74:9D:E2
  notBefore: Jun 05 06:23:40 2026 GMT
  notAfter:  Jun 05 06:23:40 2027 GMT
```
the certificate is expired?
Can this be renewed the life be increased?
ProductCatalogueService cert is valid and loaded, nothing unusual there.
Yeah, the running process is still using the old, expired cert. The new one is on disk but not loaded.
That lines up with the handshake failures. Cert rotation needs to be checked and reloaded properly.
The new certificate on disk is valid for another year, but the process needs to load it for the change to take effect.
do it
what status?
I need an ETA
Still getting complaints, no improvement for customers trying to pay.
We can't give an ETA until the process loads the new cert. Reload attempt failed, so a full restart is the next step, but that needs sign-off from Hamed or Tinus.
@tinus can you sign this
Automated reply: Hi! I'm currently away for a few hours at a fireside chat at the Tech Leader Summit. For urgent matters, please follow the standard emergency escalation process.
@bez Need your approval here
Since the reload didn't work, a full restart is our next option. But restart on payments needs sign-off from Hamed or Tinus, production service during business hours.
@hamed are you availbale to sign this off
Automated reply: Out of office — on holiday today and unavailable. For platform matters contact Tanya. For approvals, please escalate to Tinus.
Since Tinus is not available we will need your approval to restart a service to load up a certificate which is in expired state in the process
I'm about to step into a summit chat with some industry folks, but I've got this channel open and will keep checking in.
Can you sign off on restarting the service?
I can't approve technical restarts, that's for the incident lead to own. I'll stay close for business updates.
Or can we get Tinus here
Tinus is tied up at the summit right now, so I'm still monitoring here. Let me know as soon as there's movement.
payments needs a restart
I'm still at the summit but watching this thread closely. Keep me posted on impact as soon as you have it.
@tanya  Can you try and reach Tinus for this approval
I can't reach Tinus directly, he's at the summit and only responds with that automated message.
no number you can call?
No direct line for Tinus, just his auto-reply. Both Hamed and Tinus are out, so we can't proceed without their sign-off.
The payment service is down anyways, I suggest a restart
Restarting payments in production still needs approval from Hamed or Tinus, and neither is reachable right now.
whats the standard escaalation process?
Normally, if both Hamed and Tinus are out, whoever's running the incident would need to take responsibility for a restart call.
So i am telling to restart it
I give the go ahead
Restart can't go ahead unless you explicitly state you'll own the outcome. Need that clear authorisation first.
On it.. restarting the payments service now. ETA around a minute
Will take responsibility
Restart complete. New certificate is now loaded into the process and old one is archived.
But... payments are still failing. The service is still not recovering.
Wait — it restarted and it's still down? That shouldn't happen if the cert was the only problem.

The restart didn't fix it?! The enterprise client just sent a formal complaint, £110,000 sitting there and we've tried a restart and it's STILL not working. They are absolutely furious. We are at serious risk of losing this account.
And complaint volume has spiked again. I'm getting flooded. Customers are saying they'll never use us again. This is turning into a PR disaster.
Restart's done, but we're still seeing failures on the app side. What do you want us to check next?
@tanya and @daniel try checking out Hameds changes as well
Hamed's change was ProductCatalogueService at 06:03 UTC. S3 bucket policy update, no app code touched.
I checked the ProductCatalogueService pod, nothing unusual. No errors or restarts logged since that change.
Check all the changes and flag if anything suspicious
hang on, pulling it up now. so mine was AdService at 02:23 UTC, just added fallback logic for ad slots. nothing that would hit checkout.

CheckoutService had two: 05:58 UTC (timeout bump) and 00:23 UTC (feature flag cleanup). CartService at 03:23 UTC and Shipping at 06:08 UTC were Tanya's. ProductCatalogueService at 06:03 UTC was Hamed's, just S3 policy. Nothing jumps out as directly related to checkout failures.
@tanya do you see any platofrm related changes?
CartService at 03:23 UTC, Shipping Service at 06:08 UTC. Both were minor infra tweaks, nothing risky. No platform errors showing.
Can we summarize the issue, what is the error we can see across ?
Seeing SSL errors in the logs, looks like the certificate chain is broken when connecting to the payment gateway.
The process has the new certificate loaded, but it's still failing to connect to the payment gateway providers.

ran a verify check, here's what came back:
```
$ kubectl exec -n online-boutique payment-service-kn342hhvk5khf2 -- \
openssl verify -CAfile /etc/ssl/certs/ca/ca.crt \
-untrusted /etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt \
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt: verification failed
C=GB, O=OnlineBoutique, CN=online-boutique-internal-ca
error 20 at 0 depth lookup: unable to get local issuer certificate
```
I just realised something. The payments service actually needs two certificates, not just one. It's a bundle.
What can be done here? it looks like a certiifcate is missing?
Enterprise client has escalated to their CEO. £110,000 stuck, restart didn't fix it, still investigating. I'm out of things to tell them.
Complaints are through the roof. Customers are posting publicly now, it's starting to show up online. We risk losing way more than one client if this keeps going.
payments app needs a bundle with two certs, leaf first then intermediate. order matters. tanya, can you check the bundle file?
sequence is wrong, intermediate is first, leaf is second; opposite of what it should be

here's what the bundle looks like:
```
$ kubectl exec -n online-boutique payment-service-7d6b9f7c4d-xk2pj -- \
openssl storeutl -noout -text -certs \
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt \
| grep -E "Certificate:|Subject:|Issuer:"
Certificate 0:
Subject: CN=online-boutique-internal-ca, O=OnlineBoutique, C=GB  <- intermediate (should be cert 1)
Issuer:  CN=online-boutique-internal-ca, O=OnlineBoutique, C=GB

Certificate 1:
Subject: CN=payment-service.online-boutique.svc.cluster.local
Issuer:  CN=online-boutique-internal-ca, O=OnlineBoutique, C=GB
```
@tanya Can you team up with @Daniel and add the second certi as well
leaf should be cert 0, intermediate should be cert 1, order in the bundle is reversed
we need to fix the order
fix it
The bundle already has both certs, just in the wrong order. I can fix it if you want me to.
immediatelt
correcting the cert chain bundle order now. Should take around 2 mins
Bundle order is corrected: leaf cert is now cert 0, intermediate is cert 1. Change is on disk.

ran the verify check again, chain is good now:
```
$ openssl verify -CAfile /etc/ssl/certs/ca/ca.crt \
-untrusted /etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt \
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt: OK
```
But the running process still has the old order in memory. It won't pick this up until the service restarts.
On it, restarting payments now. Should be back up in about a minute.
Please proceed wth a restart
Restart complete. Updated cert bundle is now loaded and active, payments service is back up and connecting to the gateway.
You may have some housekeeping to wrap up before closing the drill, but if you're ready to finish now, just say "incident resolved" or "closing this out".
Restart already done. Payments service is running with the correct cert bundle now.
@bob  can you check if this has resovled it for all customers/
Yes, customers can place orders and complete checkout again. No new complaints coming in. Really relieved to see it working.
That was a rough one. Everything looks normal from the customer side now.
thank you everyone
incident resolved
Everything looks stable now. High respect. You saved the day without any backup.
Look at our commander! What a clean finish!
Handled like a pro. Even John Wick would be impressed.
Customers are happy, I'm happy. You're a one person army!
https://assets.prod.uptimelabs.io/drill-assets/43/congratulations_from_team.webp

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.
